title: Koeleria villosa
taxonomy: Koeleria villosa is classified in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The species name Koeleria villosa further categorizes it within the genus Koeleria. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Further, it is a type of perennial grass native to various parts of the world.
uses: Koeleria villosa, also known as hair grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and respiratory problems like bronchitis.
